title: asphodelales
intro: Asphodelales is an order of monocot flowering plants, characterized by its distinct reproductive structures and growth habits. This order includes a diverse range of families, such as Orchidaceae, which comprises orchids, Euphorbiaceae, which includes spurge and euphorbia species, and Cyperaceae, which comprises sedges and rushes. The Asphodelales order is further divided into several subgroups, each sharing similar characteristics and evolutionary relationships. The members of this order are primarily found in temperate and tropical regions around the world, and exhibit a wide range of adaptations to different environments.

families.family_desc: Xeronemataceae is a family of flowering plants in the order Poales, commonly known as the "prairie sandreed" or "sand bluestem" family.
